free_text: Nuclear charge dispersion of light-mass elements in
the fission of Th-232 by proton of medium energy
The beam current was approximately
1 uA and the time of all irradiations was 10 min.
The target consited of a thorium foil, 100 mg/cm**2
thickness,sandwiched between two aluminum guard foils,
thickness of 9 mg/cm**2. A spectroscopically pure
copper foil, 25 mg/cm**2 thickness, guarded by two
similar copper foils,was placed in the target stack
upstream with respect to the proton beam.
7.6 cm x 7.6 cm NaI(T1) scintillation crystal
coupled to a 400-channel analyser. For gamma-ray
measurements
A Beckman Wide-Beta II gas-flow proportional
counter for beta activity measurements.
.Deduced charge dispersion. Neutron emission.
Uncertainties were obtained by compounding
uncertainties in obtaining disintegration rates from
decay-curve analyses, counting efficiencies and
chemical yields.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
EN-ERR -> EN-RSL-HW. Corrections in all Subents.

From authors: Only the yield of 51 min Nb-98m was
determined. The 2.8s 98gNb reported in
Nucl.Phys.A128(1969)577 to decay by Beta-emission to
98Mo and no correction has been made for this. Thus
the measured yield is surely low.
